items.title: Custom OS installation now available on AWS DeepRacer devices
items.summary: With the stock firmware and software, developers couldn't modify their AWS DeepRacer devices to use the latest operating systems. Now, developers can upgrade or install a custom operating system (OS) by using a newly released bootloader, which extends the life of these hardware devices. In this post, we introduce the bootloader, discuss how to use it, and share links to a community distribution that uses it.
items.quality_note: Vendor/company source: useful primary signal, but not neutral proof.

items.title: Build specialized agent workflows for your business with Amazon Quick and NVIDIA NeMo Agent Toolkit
items.summary: In this post, we show how Amazon Quick can serve as the business-user front door for specialized agent workflows. We use the NVIDIA NeMo Agent Toolkit to build a supply-chain risk example that helps a planner move from an Amazon Quick dashboard and knowledge context to a guided mitigation recommendation.
items.quality_note: Vendor/company source: useful primary signal, but not neutral proof.

items.title: UK inflation cools faster than expected to 2.6% in June as food and fuel prices drop – business live
items.summary: Rolling coverage of the latest economic and financial news If ever there was need for more evidence that pubs are being squeezed particularly hard by rising inflation – JD Wetherspoon has just issued its fourth profit warning in seven months. Chairman and founder Tim Martin said in a brief update this morning: Profits for the year are likely to be below market expectations, with marginally lower sales than anticipated in the final quarter, combined with higher costs in the areas of food, labour, repairs, energy and business rates. Any dovish impulse for the Bank of England from the softer UK headline CPI will be more than offset by the firmer core reading. The June data are unlikely to alter money market bets for a hold next week or a hike in September — however, we believe markets will be surprised in September when the BoE continues to hold. Today’s data are unusually backward-looking nature given the recent re-escalation in the Middle East. Looking ahead, the inflation picture becomes more complicated. Commodity prices are climbing again as Middle East tensions intensify, and July’s energy-price-cap increase will lift household bills. Looking ahead, previous Chancellor Rachel Re

However, as households struggle to adapt to soaring summer temperatures, staff at the government-owned National Energy System Operator (Neso), tasked with keeping the lights on, have faced the kind of challenge usually reserved for the country’s coldest winter nights in recent weeks. The Danish pharmaceuticals heavyweight complained that the US group – which makes rival weight-loss treatments Zepbound and Mounjaro – “intentionally selected outdated studies” that compared Lilly’s highest doses against lower doses of Novo Nordisk’s medicines. items.title: Trump must divulge financial details about businesses in $10bn BBC lawsuit
items.summary: US judge’s ruling could open up window into first family’s multiple ventures, in drawback of lawsuit on media Donald Trump must divulge detailed financial information from his many businesses as part of his $10bn defamation lawsuit against the British Broadcasting Corporation, a federal judge ruled during a discovery hearing on Tuesday, according to news reports. The ruling by US magistrate judge Enjoliqué Lett could open a unique window into the hundreds of businesses owned by Trump’s family trust. It also highlights a potential drawback for Trump’s strategy of bludgeoning critical media with multibillion-dollar lawsuits. Continue reading...
